description: Maclean's is an active Canadian news magazine that was founded in 1905 as The Business Magazine , reporting on issues such as politics, pop culture, and current events. It is published monthly by Rogers Publishing Ltd. Its founder, publisher John Bayne Maclean, established the magazine to provide a Canadian perspective on current affairs and to "entertain but also inspire its readers". It was renamed The Busy Man's Magazine in December 1905, and began publishing articles on varied topics such as immigration, national defence, home life, women's suffrage, and fiction. Maclean renamed the magazine after himself in 1911, dropping the previous title as too evocative of a business magazine for what had become a general interest publication. Under the editorship of Peter C. Newman, the magazine switched from being a monthly general interest publication to a bi-weekly news magazine in 1975, and to a weekly newsmagazine three years later. The Internet Archive Collection contains microfilm published between 1911 and 2016. Maclean’s can be found online at: https://www.macleans.ca/ Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maclean's The ISSN is: 0024-9262 Publication History Formerly, The Business Magazine (1905) Formerly, The Busy Man’s Magazine (1905 - 1911)
subject: General Interest Periodicals, News Magazines, Canada
title: Maclean's
